infoAccordion.whatIsSurmaContent: Surma is a program designed for organizing fun assassination tournaments, maintained and developed by active members of the Helsinki University's Society of Human Hunters, which is
infoAccordion.whatIsSurmaContent2: . Surma is used to manage assassination tournaments: players share information with each other through Surma during the tournament.
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent1: In an assassination tournament, players live their everyday lives while trying to get into situations unnoticed by others, where they can apply playful weapon substitutes to other players as allowed by the rules. The tournament is played among participants in murder chains: player A's goal is to assassinate the target, player B; player B hunts player C, and so on up to player N, who waits for player A. If A successfully kills B in a way allowed by the rules, A and B contact the umpires and report what happened for scoring purposes. Now B drops out of the game, and A starts hunting C as their new target. The game is played around the clock until a predetermined time limit, at which point the game winner is determined by points. To make the game easier, players share, among other things, their calendars and home addresses with each other in Surma.
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent2: Participation in an assassination tournament begins by registering for the game through Surma, after which Surma and the tournament umpires send additional instructions by email.
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent3: Read more about player data and how it is handled in
infoAccordion.whatIsAssassinationTournamentContent4: Read more about assassination tournaments and their rules on

userForm.title: Registration
userForm.welcomeMessage: Welcome to register for the HUSH assassination tournament
userForm.description: Registration is a two-step process. First, we ask for your name, email address and phone number. This information is needed to log into Surma and for the umpires to contact you about tournament-related matters. After confirming your email address, you will be able to enter the rest of the information required in the tournament. By registering for the tournament, you accept the Helsinki University's Society of Human Hunters'
userForm.emailNotValidError: The provided email address is not valid. Try a different address
userForm.genericError: Something went wrong. Please contact the umpires

playerForm.title: Registration
playerForm.description: This form asks for information relevant to the tournament, which will be visible to your hunters. After completing the form, the umpires will confirm your registration.
playerForm.mandatoryFields: Only your alias and address are mandatory at this stage - you can fill in the other fields later and edit them during the tournament.
playerForm.calendarInfo: Calendar information should be kept up to date and sufficiently clear and detailed. You don't need to document every second, but the game becomes more fun for you and your hunters if you provide them with enough opportunities for assassination.
playerForm.infoField: NOTE! Changing the language in the middle of filling out the form will clear the form!
playerForm.safetyNotes: Safety notes (e.g. times and places outside the game)
playerForm.agreedTo: By registering for the tournament, you accept the Helsinki University's Society of Human Hunters'
playerForm.error: Something went wrong. Please contact the umpires

gdprModal.title: Short privacy policy
gdprModal.noAnalytics: We do not do analytics with your data. We only use your data for maintaining Surma and organizing the assassination tournament.
gdprModal.dataDeletion: We delete your data from Surma after the tournament.
gdprModal.dataSharing: We share data with our trusted service providers only to the extent necessary to provide the services, and we share data with players only to the extent necessary to participate in the tournament.
gdprModal.privacyPolicyButton: Actually, I want to read the full policy!

imageUpload.uploadError: Failed to upload image to the server

whatIsSurma.title: What is Surma?
whatIsSurma.content: Surma is an application for creating, managing and maintaining assassination tournaments. In assassination tournaments, players attempt to playfully "assassinate" each other using various information and within the framework of the rules of the Helsinki University's assassination game players association. By registering and participating in an assassination tournament in Surma, the user accepts the use of their information in Surma for the purposes of organizing the assassination tournament and maintaining Surma, in accordance with this privacy policy, Finnish national legislation and the European Union data protection regulation.
whatIsSurma.link: Helsinki University's assassination game players association's tournament rules

dataSources.title: Data sources
dataSources.content: User information is collected through two forms and cookies. The user fills in the first form when registering for an assassination tournament in Surma (hereinafter "registration form") and the second form after registration in Surma (hereinafter "participation form"). Registration and participation in the tournament are voluntary. Cookies about the user are created when filling out the forms and using Surma.

collectedData.registrationFormTitle: Data collected through the registration form:
registrationFormItems: Consent to process information in accordance with this privacy notice, and
Timestamp of when the user filled out the form.

items: Timestamp of the user's last login to Surma, and
Timestamp of the last time the user modified their information in Surma.

umpires.title: Umpires use the following information for internal and user-specific communication regarding the assassination tournament (consent):

umpiresPublic.title: Umpires use the following information for open and public communication on the tournament event page (consent):

umpiresAndPlayers.title: Umpires and other players use the following information to identify the user in the tournament and for playing and maintaining the tournament (consent):
items: Safety notes (e.g., times and places outside the game) and

retentionPeriod.title: Retention period
retentionPeriod.content: The user's tournament-specific anonymous alias is published on the tournament event page indefinitely, which is a separate website from Surma. All other data collected in Surma is used only during the assassination tournament and is destroyed after the assassination tournament ends.

rightToKnow.title: Right to know what information has been collected and to transfer information elsewhere
rightToKnow.content: All information collected about the user is available in JSON and CSV formats upon request. Users can request information from the registry processors. Users are asked to describe in detail whether the request concerns only Surma data or also data from our service providers. We provide the information as quickly as possible. We will notify the estimated delivery time based on the scope of the request. Users can transfer the received information to another data controller.

rightToRectify.title: Right to rectify information
rightToRectify.content: User-provided data is assumed to be correct as is. The umpires may wish to verify the accuracy of the user's information. Users can also edit the participation form information themselves in Surma except for the alias. In other cases, users can contact the registry processors.

rightToBeForotten.title: Right to be forgotten
rightToBeForotten.content: Users can be forgotten at any time by submitting a request to the registry processors. If the user's data is deleted, their tournament participation is also canceled.

rightToObject.title: Right to object or restrict data processing
rightToObject.content: Users can object to or restrict the processing of their personal information as they wish for the purposes mentioned in this privacy notice by submitting a request to the registry processors. Restricting or objecting to the processing of personal information will result in the cancellation of the user's tournament participation and the retention of information until the end of the tournament.

thirdParties.title: Third parties
thirdParties.intro: The EU data protection regulation grants users several rights that allow users to often determine data processing by third parties. Below is more information about data processing by third parties in Surma.
thirdParties.dataTransfer: Information collected in Surma is transferred to third parties through the service providers used. Information is transferred to service providers only to the extent they need to provide their services for the purposes defined in this privacy notice. Service providers have been selected carefully, taking into account their responsibility regarding data protection.
thirdParties.playerData: Information collected in Surma is transferred to other users only to the extent they need it to participate in the tournament.
thirdParties.databaseInfo: User information is stored in Surma's PostgreSQL database, which is maintained for free on Vercel. Vercel is a US-owned company. Surma's database server is located in Washington, USA.
thirdParties.vercelLink: Vercel's, the provider of Surma's database server, privacy policy in English
thirdParties.hostingInfo: Surma's user interface server is maintained through Suncomet, a Finnish-owned company. Suncomet's servers are located around the world, but our website is primarily on a C1 server.
thirdParties.sunCometLink: Suncomet's, the provider of Surma's user interface and website maintenance, server information in Finnish
thirdParties.eventPageInfo: The tournament event page is published as part of HYS's website for free on WordPress.org, which is open-source software. WordPress's server is maintained through Suncomet, a Finnish-owned company. WordPress Foundation, a non-profit organization in the United States, owns WordPress and ensures the software's open availability.
thirdParties.wordPressLink: WordPress', the provider of the website editing software, privacy policy in English
thirdParties.dataDisclosure: User information is not disclosed to other third parties except for legal reasons:
disclosureReasons: To comply with applicable laws and/or court orders;
To detect, prevent or handle misuse, security risks or technical issues.

contact.title: Contact information
contact.intro: Questions about the registry and Surma's issues are directed to the Surma developers. Questions about the tournament are directed to the umpires.
contact.dataControllerOrg: Helsinki University's assassination game players association
contact.complaint: If you believe that the processing of your personal information violates data protection legislation, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority responsible for the data controller's processing of personal information. In Finland, a complaint is made to the data protection ombudsman in accordance with their instructions. You can find more information on
